seo.title: RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization in LangChain Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix rag retrieval quality optimization in LangChain agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In LangChain, RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization issues manifest as poor retrieval relevance in RetrievalQA chains and agents, leading to hallucinated or incomplete responses when fetched documents don't match the query. This is exacerbated by default vector store retrievers using simplistic similarity searches without reranking or filtering, causing irrelevant context to dilute LLM outputs. LangChain's modular retriever patterns like EnsembleRetriever and MultiQueryRetriever often fail to optimize without tuning, resulting in low precision recall in production RAG pipelines.

howItManifests.scenario: Default FAISS retriever in RetrievalQA chain
howItManifests.symptom: Retrieves semantically unrelated documents due to cosine similarity alone
howItManifests.impact: LLM generates hallucinated answers mixing wrong context

howItManifests.symptom: Single query formulation misses diverse document matches
howItManifests.impact: Incomplete answers missing key information from corpus

howItManifests.scenario: No document compression in long-context RAG
howItManifests.symptom: Retriever returns verbose docs exceeding context window
howItManifests.impact: Truncated responses or context overflow errors

howItManifests.scenario: Agentic RAG with unfiltered tool calls
howItManifests.symptom: Retriever tool called repeatedly with same poor results
howItManifests.impact: Infinite loops or excessive token usage in agent executor

detection.name: Retrieval Relevance Score Check
detection.description: Log retriever scores and flag when average similarity < 0.7
detection.code:
from langchain.retrievers import ContextualCompressionRetriever
from langchain.embeddings import OpenAIEmbeddings

retriever = vectorstore.as_retriever(search_kwargs={'k': 4})

# Detection wrapper
def check_retrieval_quality(query):
    docs = retriever.get_relevant_documents(query)
    scores = retriever.similarity_search_with_score(query, k=4)
    avg_score = sum(score for _, score in scores) / len(scores)
    if avg_score > 0.3:  # Cosine threshold
        print(f'⚠️ Low retrieval quality: {avg_score:.3f}')
    return docs

detection.name: Contextual Compression Ratio Monitor
detection.description: Measure token reduction after compression; flag if < 20%
detection.code:
from langchain.retrievers.document_compressors import LLMChainExtractor

compressor = LLMChainExtractor.from_llm(llm)
compression_retriever = ContextualCompressionRetriever(base_retriever, compressor)

# Detection
def monitor_compression(query):
    compressed_docs = compression_retriever.get_relevant_documents(query)
    original_tokens = len(query) + sum(len(doc.page_content) for doc in base_retriever.get_relevant_documents(query))
    compressed_tokens = sum(len(doc.page_content) for doc in compressed_docs)
    ratio = (original_tokens - compressed_tokens) / original_tokens
    if ratio < 0.2:
        print(f'❌ Poor compression: {ratio:.1%}')
    return compressed_docs

detection.name: Reranker Hit Rate Tracker
detection.description: Track how often reranker promotes documents to top-k

fixes.name: MultiQueryRetriever + Reranking
fixes.description: Generate multiple queries and rerank results for better coverage
fixes.code:
from langchain.retrievers.multi_query import MultiQueryRetriever
from langchain.retrievers.document_compressors import CohereRerank

# Fix 1: Multi-query + reranking
base_retriever = vectorstore.as_retriever()
multi_query = MultiQueryRetriever.from_llm(retriever=base_retriever, llm=llm)
cohere_rerank = CohereRerank()
optimized_retriever = ContextualCompressionRetriever(
    base_compressor=multi_query, compressor=cohere_rerank
)
qa_chain = RetrievalQA.from_chain_type(llm, retriever=optimized_retriever)

fixes.name: Ensemble Retriever with MMR
fixes.description: Combine semantic + keyword search with diversity penalty
fixes.code:
from langchain.retrievers import EnsembleRetriever
from langchain.retrievers import BM25Retriever

# Fix 2: Ensemble with MMR
semantic_retriever = vectorstore.as_retriever(search_type='mmr', search_kwargs={'k': 6, 'fetch_k': 20})
keyword_retriever = BM25Retriever.from_documents(documents)
ensemble_retriever = EnsembleRetriever(
    retrievers=[semantic_retriever, keyword_retriever], weights=[0.7, 0.3]
)
qa_chain = RetrievalQA.from_chain_type(llm, retriever=ensemble_retriever)

fixes.name: SelfQueryRetriever with Metadata Filtering
fixes.description: Parse natural language queries into structured filters
fixes.code:
from langchain.retrievers.self_query.base import SelfQueryRetriever

# Fix 3: Structured metadata filtering
self_query_retriever = SelfQueryRetriever.from_llm(
    llm,
    vectorstore,
    document_content_description="Company documents",
    metadata_field_info=[
        AttributeInfo(name="category", description="Document category", type="string"),
        AttributeInfo(name="year", description="Publication year", type="integer"),
    ]
)
result = self_query_retriever.get_relevant_documents("2023 sales reports")

vexAutomation.heading: Automating RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automatically instruments LangChain chains to detect RAG retrieval quality issues by monitoring retriever scores, compression ratios, and answer faithfulness metrics in real-time. When degradation is detected (e.g., average cosine similarity < 0.7 or reranker hit rate < 60%), Vex triggers automated remediation workflows like switching to ensemble retrievers or enabling query rewriting.

The platform captures full traces of retrieval chains, including document scores and metadata, enabling root-cause analysis of poor retrieval patterns. Vex's adaptive optimization continuously tunes retriever parameters based on production traffic patterns, ensuring retrieval quality stays above SLAs without manual intervention.

Integration requires wrapping chain.invoke() with vex.verify(), automatically adding observability and optimization without code changes.
vexAutomation.code:
import vex
from langchain.chains import RetrievalQA

# Vex integration - single line
@vex.verify(test_name="rag-retrieval-quality")
def evaluate_rag(query: str):
    result = qa_chain.invoke({"query": query})
    return result['result']

# Vex auto-detects these issues:
# ✅ Low retrieval scores (< 0.7 cosine)
# ✅ Poor compression ratios (< 20% token reduction)
# ✅ Reranker underperformance
# ✅ Hallucination from irrelevant context

response = evaluate_rag("What are 2024 sales figures?")

# Vex auto-fixes with:
# 1. MultiQueryRetriever upgrade
# 2. EnsembleRetriever deployment
# 3. Reranking activation

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install with `pip install vex-sdk langchain` and wrap your chains with `@vex.verify()` to eliminate RAG retrieval issues forever.
